essence: Shapes and restores stone structures with craftsmanship, precision, and deep respect for traditional building techniques.

identity.title: Stonemason
identity.summary: Cuts, shapes, and installs natural stone for buildings, monuments, and restoration projects, combining traditional craft with modern tools.

salary.notes: Heritage restoration specialists and highly skilled masons can exceed £50k–£65k+ depending on project scale and expertise.

qualifications: Apprenticeship or Level 2/3 Diploma in Stonemasonry
Knowledge of stone types, tools, and carving techniques
Understanding of restoration and conservation methods
Strong hand‑eye coordination and physical stamina

lifestyle.description: Stonemasons work on construction sites, workshops, and heritage restoration projects, shaping stone for walls, facades, monuments, and architectural details. Days involve cutting and carving stone, reading technical drawings, repairing historic structures, and working with both hand tools and machinery. The role is physically demanding and requires patience, precision, and artistic skill. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy craftsmanship, tradition, and creating work that lasts for centuries.

habits: Works patiently with precision and care
Follows safety procedures around heavy materials
Studies traditional and modern masonry techniques
